what: Native Quantized Matrix Products and LLM Inference over Rfmalloc Tensors
title: Native Quantized Matrix Products and LLM Inference over Rfmalloc Tensors
text: Rllm composition layer Rfmalloc --core array stack: wires Rfmalloc (file-backed, memory-mapped storage), Rgguf (GGUF model files typed storage views) Rggml (vendored GGML compute runtime-SIMD-dispatched quantized kernels) LLM inference - quantized weights stay GGUF encoding, memory-mapped disk, never decoded double compute path. first composition codec-aware matrix-product backend: dense %*% quantized_fmalloc_tensor contracts encoded blocks directly GGML registering Rfmalloc’s backend registry. second normalizes GGUF metadata semantic plans composes plans forward graphs, state, embeddings byte-level generation typed storage. Llama, Qwen3.5, LFM2MoE EmbeddingGemma model probes operator vocabulary rather C++ subclasses.

title: Native Quantized Matrix Products and LLM Inference over Rfmalloc Tensors
code: library(Rllm)   # registers + selects the ggml backend  rt <- Rfmalloc::open_fmalloc(tempfile(fileext = ".bin")) set.seed(1) W  <- matrix(rnorm(512 * 8, sd = 0.4), nrow = 512) Wq <- rllm_quantize_tensor(W, "q4_k", runtime = rt)  # 4.5 bits/weight, mmap'd X  <- matrix(rnorm(4 * 512), nrow = 4) Y  <- X %*% Wq                                       # GGML SIMD, no f64 decode sqrt(sum((Y - X %*% W)^2) / sum((X %*% W)^2))        # q4_k-accurate #> [1] 0.09047353

title: Native Quantized Matrix Products and LLM Inference over Rfmalloc Tensors
text: model /O boundary token ids raw bytes - never character vectors core. rllm_encode()/rllm_decode() edge codecs built GGUF’s byte-level BPE metadata (external tokenizer); rawToChar() caller’s interpretation. boundary serves byte-level, vision, pure-codec models later. evaluated transcript comes LFM2.5-8B-A1B Q4_K_M. Set RLLM_README_GGUF rendering Rmd. absent model skips evaluation; document substitute toy model recorded success text. knitr cache keyed model file’s size modification time.
code: local({   backing <- tempfile(fileext = ".bin")   rt <- Rfmalloc::open_fmalloc(backing, mode = "scratch", size_gb = 2)   on.exit({     Rfmalloc::cleanup_fmalloc(rt)     unlink(backing)   }, add = TRUE)    model_path <- Sys.getenv(     "RLLM_README_GGUF",     "LFM2.5-8B-A1B-Q4_K_M.gguf"   )   model <- rllm_gguf_model(model_path, runtime = rt)   gen <- rllm_generate(     model,     charToRaw("The capital of France is"),     n_new = 16L,     runtime = rt   )   rawToChar(gen$raw) }) #> [1] " the city of Paris. city of Paris is the capital of France. Both statements"

title: Native Quantized Matrix Products and LLM Inference over Rfmalloc Tensors
text: architecture described ordinary R functions. rllm_module() gives forward function persistent identity, base pipe records dataflow, rllm_residual() records branches, rllm_tap() names intermediate outputs rllm_loop() preserves shared recurrence instead unrolling . Freezing trace rllm_program() removes builder environment leaves serializable data. rllm_inputs() starts trace several typed inputs, operator’s outputs specification keeps several named results graph. Qwen3.5 plan first deliberately heterogeneous model probe. metadata tensor directory Ternary Bonsai 27B normalize one R data AST 48 gated-delta recurrent blocks, 16 gated-attention blocks, two kinds persistent state 851 typed parameter bindings. official group-64 q2_0 weights remain storage-codec concern. recurrent attention mixture remains architecture concern. Neither becomes model-family branch C++. operators lower Rggml, including MRoPE, head-interleaved query gates, causal convolution recurrent matrix state. Whole-batch incremental execution pinned pure-R equation oracle. real checkpoint, token-zero forward pass produces 248,320 logits bit--bit identically upstream llama.cpp built corresponding portable CPU backend. pinned GGML release q2_0 CUDA Vulkan matrix-product kernel, ternary checkpoint executes CPU. Qwen3.5 graph can use CUDA weights use codec backend supports. program interpreter executes dataflow structured control flow explicit table R operator lowerings. dense reference vocabulary pins Tiny Recursive Model evaluation direct R recurrence: z_H z_L cross three nested symbolic loops one reasoning module stays shared. full attention SwiGLU TRM topology also serializable, Samsung checkpoint importer native bidirectional-attention lowering yet exist. rllm_execute() semantic oracle lowering harness; replace GGUF path rllm_forward(). native lowerer implements operators exercised llama, Qwen3.5, LFM2MoE EmbeddingGemma. ESM-2 Evo 2 remain executable-frontier probes, programs longer caricatures. ESM-2 8M program records typed token padding inputs, six attention layers returning hidden states attention maps, tied embeddings, representation taps contact head maps. Evo 2 7B program records complete 32-layer schedule: 27 short, medium long Hyena cascades distinct FIR IIR state, interleaved five causal-attention layers. Missing checkpoint importers numerical lowerings fail explicitly; model-family branches hidden C++. Rtinycc plausible prototype target generated C reference operators ABI glue. can compile lowering memory test R semantics hot operators promoted Rggml. treated substitute GGML’s tuned tensor kernels. recorded run SmolLM2-135M Q4_K_M (30 layers, grouped-query attention, q5_0-dominant quantization mix) used 12-token prompt 128 greedy decode tokens. median three complete generations 40.2 tokens/s CPU 69.7 tokens/s RTX 5050 CUDA path one-time weight upload. rllm_gguf_model() reads hyperparameters borrows every weight’s exact read-span. weight copied Rfmalloc work file. CPU, rllm_forward() points GGML tensors bytes assembling RMSNorm, RoPE, causal grouped-query attention SwiGLU Rggml’s C-callables; Rllm links private GGML copy. rllm_kv_cache() allocates one key value slab per layer plain R memory fmalloc storage. latter makes cache file-backed, evictable open different cache codec without changing graph. rllm_generate() prefills cache performs EOS-aware decoding one token time.
code: d <- "hidden" norm <- rllm_parameter("encoder.norm.weight", d)  encoder_block <- rllm_module("encoder.block", function(x) {   x |>     rllm_residual(function(branch) {       branch |>         rllm_norm(norm) |>         rllm_op(           "attention",           mask = list(type = "bidirectional")         )     }) })  program <- rllm_input(   "embedding", c(feature = d, sequence = "n_token") ) |>   encoder_block() |>   rllm_tap("per_token") |>   rllm_pool("mean") |>   rllm_program("encoder")  program #> <rllm_program encoder: 5 nodes, 1 parameters, 2 outputs; add=1/attention=1/input=1/pool=1/rms_norm=1>

title: Native Quantized Matrix Products and LLM Inference over Rfmalloc Tensors
text: forward pass pinned pure-R implementations arithmetic llama, Qwen3.5, LFM2MoE EmbeddingGemma. Hermetic f32 models require incremental state equal whole-batch execution every position. Qwen3.5 also compared every real-model logit upstream build using portable CPU arithmetic. real quantized MoE, changing batch width can select different GGML GEMV GEMM kernel; tiny router-score rounding may cross top-k boundary, real-file test pins chosen token logit direction generation checked upstream continuation. Every codec decoder must bit-identical GGML’s type-traits to_float, cross-validation caught Q4_K bug earlier partial C port. byte boundary, rllm_decode(rllm_encode(x)) must always equal x. hermetic tests write synthetic GGUF models test time Rgguf’s writer. RLLM_TEST_GGUF=<path> exercises real generation model, RLLM_EMBEDDING_GGUF=<path> compares 300M EmbeddingGemma Q8_0 probe upstream llama.cpp reference. RLLM_QWEN35_GGUF=<path> executes real hybrid checkpoint; pairing RLLM_QWEN35_REFERENCE=<f32.bin> checks portable upstream logit transcript bit--bit.

title: Native Quantized Matrix Products and LLM Inference over Rfmalloc Tensors
text: CPU path borrows GGUF mapping directly uses GGML’s runtime-dispatched quantized kernels: AVX2 selected CPUID x86 mandatory NEON baseline aarch64, non-portable flags recorded package. Dense products remain available R’s BLAS. optional CUDA path executes graph. model-owned context uploads codec-native weights retains device memory; subsequent forward passes move mutable inputs, cache state logits Rggml’s backend-neutral transfer interface. host GGUF mapping remains authoritative storage, device residency execution decision rather another model format. Vulkan also ships Rggml, Rllm select . rig suite checks whole-batch cached CUDA logits, plain fmalloc cache slabs, cache handoff directions CPU CUDA. Persistent allocation missing acceleration. measured GGML scheduler experiment reduced CUDA decode 69.7 63.7 tokens/s, CUDA graph capture reached 62.6 attention extent graph properties change every token. experiments removed default path. Stable graph shapes device-resident mutable KV cache remain open execution questions; storage GGUF interfaces need fork either.

title: Native Quantized Matrix Products and LLM Inference over Rfmalloc Tensors
text: r-universe: via GitHub subdir ref: pak::pak("sounkou-bioinfo/Rfmalloc/packages/Rllm"). CPU stack targets Linux, macOS Windows; optional CUDA build Unix-like. GPL (>= 2).
code: install.packages("Rllm",   repos = c("https://sounkou-bioinfo.r-universe.dev", getOption("repos")))

what: Rllm: Native Quantized Matrix Products and LLM Inference over Rfmalloc — Rllm-package
title: Rllm: Native Quantized Matrix Products and LLM Inference over Rfmalloc — Rllm-package
text: Rllm composition layer Rfmalloc ecosystem. registers Rggml (vendored GGML build runtime-SIMD-dispatched quantized kernels) codec-aware matrix-multiply backend Rfmalloc, products file-backed quantized tensors run natively quantized space rather decoded double first. Together Rgguf (exposes GGUF model weights borrowed typed spans) lets larger--memory model's linear layers multiply GGML's SIMD-accelerated dot kernels, zero-copy original GGUF mapping.

title: Rllm: Native Quantized Matrix Products and LLM Inference over Rfmalloc — Rllm-package
text: Loading package registers selects "ggml" backend; toggle rllm_use_ggml(). Build quantized tensors rllm_quantize_tensor().

title: Rllm: Native Quantized Matrix Products and LLM Inference over Rfmalloc — Rllm-package
text: Maintainer: Sounkou Mahamane Toure sounkoutoure@gmail.com Authors: Sounkou Mahamane Toure sounkoutoure@gmail.com

what: Convert between token ids and raw bytes — rllm_decode
title: Convert between token ids and raw bytes — rllm_decode
text: ids <-> bytes edge codecs bytes-boundary API, built model's GGUF tokenizer metadata (byte-level BPE, tokenizer.ggml.model == "gpt2"). rllm_decode() maps ids exact bytes stand . rllm_encode() byte-pair-encodes bytes ids using file's merge ranks; applies merges without GPT-2's regex pre-tokenizer, tokenization may occasionally differ llama.cpp's canonical split - every output still valid encoding input (rllm_decode(rllm_encode(x)) always x).

title: Convert between token ids and raw bytes — rllm_decode
code: rllm_decode(model, ids)  rllm_encode(model, x)

title: Convert between token ids and raw bytes — rllm_decode
text: model rllm_model whose GGUF carries byte-level BPE tokenizer. ids Integer vector 0-based token ids. x raw vector (single string, converted charToRaw()).

title: Convert between token ids and raw bytes — rllm_decode
text: rllm_decode(): raw vector. rllm_encode(): integer vector 0-based token ids.

what: Compute one pooled sequence embedding — rllm_embed
title: Compute one pooled sequence embedding — rllm_embed
text: Lowers embedding model's semantic plan complete token sequence. Bidirectional symmetric-window attention evaluated without decode cache, plan's pooling projection pipeline produces one vector. Quantized weights remain encoded mapped GGUF file.

title: Compute one pooled sequence embedding — rllm_embed
code: rllm_embed(model, tokens, normalize = TRUE, backend = c("cpu", "cuda"))

title: Compute one pooled sequence embedding — rllm_embed
text: model embedding rllm_model rllm_gguf_model(). tokens Integer vector 0-based token ids. normalize Whether return unit-length vector. backend Compute backend. "cpu" uses mapped weights directly; "cuda" requires CUDA-enabled Rggml installation.

title: Compute one pooled sequence embedding — rllm_embed
text: numeric vector whose length model$plan$output$dimension.

title: Compute one pooled sequence embedding — rllm_embed
text: Tokenization deliberately separate model execution. Supply model's 0-based token ids, including BOS EOS tokens required tokenizer.

what: Execute a data-only architecture program — rllm_execute
title: Execute a data-only architecture program — rllm_execute
text: rllm_execute() interprets dataflow structured loops rllm_program(). Semantic operators ordinary R functions supplied operators; small dense reference vocabulary provided arithmetic shared architecture probes. reference lowering harness, GGUF execution path used rllm_forward().

title: Execute a data-only architecture program — rllm_execute
code: rllm_execute(   program,   inputs,   parameters = list(),   counts = list(),   operators = list() )

title: Execute a data-only architecture program — rllm_execute
text: program data-rllm_program. inputs named list input values. Extra entries remain available operator functions context$inputs. parameters named list containing value every declared program parameter. counts named list atomic vector resolving symbolic loop counts symbolic integer attributes. operators named list semantic operator functions.

title: Execute a data-only architecture program — rllm_execute
text: named list containing every program output tap.

title: Execute a data-only architecture program — rllm_execute
text: operator function called four named arguments: inputs, list values arriving node; attributes, node's data-attributes; parameters, named parameter values; context, list containing current node program, root local inputs, symbolic counts, enclosing loop iterations. Operator functions control representation results, lowering may preserve mapped device-backed value rather materializing R. built-dense reference operators add, linear, rms_norm, layer_norm, pool, gelu, silu, sigmoid, tanh, broadcast_initial, select_first_token, drop_puzzle_prefix swiglu. Entries operators replace built-ins name.

what: Lower a semantic model plan and return its logits — rllm_forward
title: Lower a semantic model plan and return its logits — rllm_forward
text: Lowers model's inspectable rllm_plan() GGML graph memory-mapped weights computes chosen backend. operator vocabulary includes causal gated attention, gated-delta recurrence, short convolution, dense gated products sparse routed experts. Quantized weights contracted natively encoded form - never decoded double. CPU backend borrows mapped bytes directly. first use, CUDA backend creates model-owned context uploads codec-native weights . Later passes reuse resident weights; mutable inputs, cache slabs logits move Rggml's transfer API.

title: Lower a semantic model plan and return its logits — rllm_forward
code: rllm_forward(model, tokens, cache = NULL, backend = c("cpu", "cuda"))

title: Lower a semantic model plan and return its logits — rllm_forward
text: model rllm_model rllm_gguf_model(). tokens Integer vector 0-based token ids (GGUF vocab). cache Optional rllm_kv_cache() incremental decoding. backend Compute backend. "cpu" zero-copy mmap path; "cuda" requires Rggml installed ---cuda visible NVIDIA device.

title: Lower a semantic model plan and return its logits — rllm_forward
text: numeric matrix logits, dim c(n_vocab, length(tokens)): column scores token following position .

title: Lower a semantic model plan and return its logits — rllm_forward
text: Without cache, graph evaluates complete token batch zero state. rllm_kv_cache(), pass advances every state declared plan, including key/value, convolution gated-delta state, advances cache$n_past. incremental-decoding path: prefill prompt, feed one token time.

what: Generate tokens with a KV cache (greedy or sampled) — rllm_generate
title: Generate tokens with a KV cache (greedy or sampled) — rllm_generate
text: bytes-/bytes-generation entry point: prefills KV cache prompt (ids raw bytes), decodes one token per step - step single-token rllm_forward() cache, full re-forward. Stops n_new tokens model's EOS token. Decoding greedy default (temperature = 0); set positive temperature temperature-scaled sampling, optionally narrowed top_k /top_p.

title: Generate tokens with a KV cache (greedy or sampled) — rllm_generate
code: rllm_generate(   model,   prompt,   n_new = 32L,   temperature = 0,   top_k = 0L,   top_p = 1,   seed = NULL,   cache = NULL,   runtime = NULL,   backend = c("cpu", "cuda") )

title: Generate tokens with a KV cache (greedy or sampled) — rllm_generate
text: model rllm_model rllm_gguf_model(). prompt Integer vector 0-based token ids, raw vector (encoded rllm_encode(); single string converted via charToRaw() convenience). textual prompt start sequence receives GGUF tokenizer's BOS token one declared. Integer prompts remain exact never modified. n_new Maximum number tokens generate. temperature Sampling temperature. 0 (default) greedy/argmax; larger values flatten distribution (diverse). top_k Keep top_k highest-logit tokens sampling (0, default, disables cutoff). Ignored greedy. top_p Nucleus sampling: keep smallest set tokens whose probabilities sum least top_p (1, default, disables ). Ignored greedy. seed Optional integer seed, reproducible sampling. cache Optional rllm_kv_cache() continue ; default fresh cache sized length(prompt) + n_new created. runtime Optional Rfmalloc::open_fmalloc() runtime cache slabs (file-backed cache). backend Compute backend passed rllm_forward(). "cuda" requires CUDA-enabled Rggml build.

title: Generate tokens with a KV cache (greedy or sampled) — rllm_generate
text: list ids (prompt + generated, 0-based), new_ids (generated tokens), raw (generated tokens decoded bytes, NULL model tokenizer metadata).

what: Load a GGUF model as a semantic execution plan and borrowed weights — rllm_gguf_model
title: Load a GGUF model as a semantic execution plan and borrowed weights — rllm_gguf_model
text: Normalizes model-family metadata typed rllm_plan(), validates every tensor role shape, borrows required weight original GGUF mapping. Quantized floating-point payloads keep -disk encoding; second weight store created.

title: Load a GGUF model as a semantic execution plan and borrowed weights — rllm_gguf_model
code: rllm_gguf_model(path, runtime = NULL, rope_mode = NULL)

title: Load a GGUF model as a semantic execution plan and borrowed weights — rllm_gguf_model
text: path Path GGUF file. runtime Rfmalloc::open_fmalloc() runtime attached borrowed tensor views, NULL use default established Rfmalloc::init_fmalloc(). supplies allocation context operations produce fmalloc results; weight bytes remain GGUF mapping. rope_mode Optional RoPE override: 0 normal/interleaved 2 NEOX. architecture plan supplies default.

title: Load a GGUF model as a semantic execution plan and borrowed weights — rllm_gguf_model
text: object class rllm_model containing hyperparameters, borrowed weight payloads, tokenizer metadata, model-owned backend contexts created lazily rllm_forward().

title: Load a GGUF model as a semantic execution plan and borrowed weights — rllm_gguf_model
text: Architecture definitions data ASTs rather native model-family branches. registered plans cover llama, Qwen3.5, LFM2MoE EmbeddingGemma. Models tied embeddings reuse token_embd.weight output projection.

what: Build a typed model program with ordinary R functions and pipes — rllm_input
title: Build a typed model program with ordinary R functions and pipes — rllm_input
text: functions form R-facing architecture language. program traced ordinary R calls, including functions returned rllm_module(), residual branches structured loops. Calling rllm_program() freezes trace data : nodes, tensor parameters, shapes, module paths named outputs. resulting object contains R closures, environments backend pointers.

title: Build a typed model program with ordinary R functions and pipes — rllm_input
code: rllm_input(name, shape, dtype = "f32")  rllm_inputs(..., .dtype = "f32", .name = NULL)  rllm_parameter(name, shape, dtype = NULL, role = NULL)  rllm_module(name, forward)  rllm_op(x, op, ..., output_shape = NULL, output_dtype = NULL, outputs = NULL)  rllm_program(x, name = NULL)

title: Build a typed model program with ordinary R functions and pipes — rllm_input
text: name non-empty input, parameter, module, loop program name. shape atomic vector describing axes. Dimensions may positive numbers symbolic character strings. dtype Storage value type. ... Named, data-operator attributes, arguments passed module's forward function. .dtype One dtype inputs, named character vector one dtype per input. .name Program-builder name multiple-input trace. role Optional semantic role parameter. forward ordinary R function whose first argument traced value. body may use base R's |> pipe. x traced rllm_value, named list traced values multi-input operator, model plan, loaded model, GGUF path. op non-empty semantic operator name. output_shape Optional result shape. input shape retained omitted. output_dtype Optional result type. input type retained omitted. outputs Optional named output specifications operator multiple results. entry either shape list shape optional dtype. combined output_shape output_dtype.

title: Build a typed model program with ordinary R functions and pipes — rllm_input
text: rllm_input() single-result operators return traced rllm_value. rllm_inputs() multiple-result operators return named lists traced values. rllm_parameter() returns data-tensor reference, rllm_module() returns callable R function, rllm_program() returns data-rllm_program.

title: Build a typed model program with ordinary R functions and pipes — rllm_input
text: architecture language, second tensor runtime. Operators describe semantics; backend may lower operators implements must reject rest explicitly.

what: Create plan-shaped state for incremental decoding — rllm_kv_cache
title: Create plan-shaped state for incremental decoding — rllm_kv_cache
text: Allocates persistent state declared model plan. Attention layers receive key/value slabs, short-convolution layers receive causal history, gated-delta layers receive convolution history recurrent matrices. State plain R memory default Rfmalloc-backed runtime supplied.

title: Create plan-shaped state for incremental decoding — rllm_kv_cache
code: rllm_kv_cache(model, n_ctx = 512L, runtime = NULL)

title: Create plan-shaped state for incremental decoding — rllm_kv_cache
text: model rllm_model rllm_gguf_model(). n_ctx Maximum number positions cache can hold. runtime Optional Rfmalloc::open_fmalloc() runtime slabs.

title: Create plan-shaped state for incremental decoding — rllm_kv_cache
text: environment class rllm_kv_cache per-layer k, v, conv recurrent state, plus n_ctx n_past.

title: Create plan-shaped state for incremental decoding — rllm_kv_cache
text: returned object environment, rllm_forward() can advance n_past reference.

what: Typed linear, normalization, attention and pooling operators — rllm_linear
title: Typed linear, normalization, attention and pooling operators — rllm_linear
text: convenient typed constructors rllm_op(). keep surface close R module's forward method recording explicit parameter identities semantic attributes frozen program.

title: Typed linear, normalization, attention and pooling operators — rllm_linear
code: rllm_linear(x, weight, bias = NULL)  rllm_norm(x, weight, kind = c("rms", "layer"), eps = 1e-05, bias = NULL)  rllm_attention(   x,   query,   key,   value,   output,   heads,   kv_heads = heads,   query_norm = NULL,   key_norm = NULL,   rope = NULL,   mask = list(type = "causal"),   scale = NULL )  rllm_pool(x, kind = c("mean", "cls", "none"))

title: Typed linear, normalization, attention and pooling operators — rllm_linear
text: x traced rllm_value. weight, bias Tensor references rllm_parameter(). bias optional. kind Operator kind. eps Normalization epsilon. query, key, value, output Projection parameters attention. heads, kv_heads Query key/value head counts. query_norm, key_norm Optional per-head normalization parameters. rope, mask, scale Data-attention specifications.

title: Typed linear, normalization, attention and pooling operators — rllm_linear
text: traced rllm_value.

what: Trace a structured, shared recurrence — rllm_loop
title: Trace a structured, shared recurrence — rllm_loop
text: Unlike R loop, rllm_loop() unroll body. body traced nested data-program retained loop node. single value supports pipe syntax. named list supports multiple carried values, including nested recurrences shared parameters.

title: Trace a structured, shared recurrence — rllm_loop
code: rllm_loop(x, times, body, name = "loop")

title: Trace a structured, shared recurrence — rllm_loop
text: x traced value named list values one program. times positive integer one symbolic count. body function returning value structure x. name Loop name.

title: Trace a structured, shared recurrence — rllm_loop
text: traced value, named list traced values x list.

what: Inspect the semantic execution plan of a GGUF model — rllm_plan
title: Inspect the semantic execution plan of a GGUF model — rllm_plan
text: Rllm normalizes model-family metadata small data AST borrows weight payload. plan names tensor roles, resolved shapes, layer operators, feed-forward operators, persistent state output projection. contains data : R closures backend objects.

title: Inspect the semantic execution plan of a GGUF model — rllm_plan
code: rllm_plan(x)

title: Inspect the semantic execution plan of a GGUF model — rllm_plan
text: x rllm_model path GGUF file.

title: Inspect the semantic execution plan of a GGUF model — rllm_plan
text: inspectable object class rllm_plan.

what: Quantize a matrix into an Rfmalloc-backed quantized tensor — rllm_quantize_tensor
title: Quantize a matrix into an Rfmalloc-backed quantized tensor — rllm_quantize_tensor
text: Encodes dense numeric matrix GGUF quantized block format stores compressed payload Rfmalloc-backed (file-backed, memory-mapped) storage, returning fmalloc_tensor. write-side counterpart Rgguf's gguf_tensor(..., = "native"): resulting tensor keeps quantized storage density , multiplied right-hand operand dense %*% tensor ggml backend active (see rllm_use_ggml()), contracted natively quantized space GGML's SIMD-dispatched kernels without ever decoded double.

title: Quantize a matrix into an Rfmalloc-backed quantized tensor — rllm_quantize_tensor
code: rllm_quantize_tensor(x, dtype = "q4_k", runtime = NULL)

title: Quantize a matrix into an Rfmalloc-backed quantized tensor — rllm_quantize_tensor
text: x numeric matrix quantize. dtype Target quantized codec, one "q4_0", "q4_1", "q5_0", "q5_1", "q8_0", "q2_0", "q2_k", "q3_k", "q4_k" (default), "q5_k", "q6_k". runtime Optional Rfmalloc runtime handle (see Rfmalloc::open_fmalloc()); NULL, Rfmalloc's default runtime used.

title: Quantize a matrix into an Rfmalloc-backed quantized tensor — rllm_quantize_tensor
text: fmalloc_tensor given dtype dim(x).

title: Quantize a matrix into an Rfmalloc-backed quantized tensor — rllm_quantize_tensor
text: number rows x quantized (per-row) dimension must multiple codec's block size: 256 K-quants ("q2_k", "q3_k", "q4_k", "q5_k", "q6_k") 32 "q4_0", "q4_1", "q5_0", "q5_1", "q8_0"; "q2_0" uses GGML's group-64 ternary blocks.

title: Quantize a matrix into an Rfmalloc-backed quantized tensor — rllm_quantize_tensor
code: rt <- Rfmalloc::open_fmalloc(tempfile(fileext = ".bin")) set.seed(1) W <- matrix(rnorm(256 * 4, sd = 0.4), nrow = 256)  # 256 must divide nrow Wt <- rllm_quantize_tensor(W, "q4_k", runtime = rt) X <- matrix(rnorm(3 * 256), nrow = 3)              # 3 x 256 Y <- X %*% Wt                                      # native quantized product dim(Y) #> [1] 3 4

what: Enable or disable the ggml quantized matmul backend — rllm_use_ggml
title: Enable or disable the ggml quantized matmul backend — rllm_use_ggml
text: Rllm registers Rggml Rfmalloc codec-aware matrix-multiply backend named "ggml" selects load. active, products quantized fmalloc_tensors (types "q4_0", "q4_1", "q8_0", "q2_0", "q2_k", "q4_k", "q6_k") tensor right-hand operand (dense %*% tensor) computed ggml quantized space, contracting weight row GGML's SIMD-dispatched dot kernel, dense operand quantized fly. products (tensor left, non-quantized codecs) declined fall back Rfmalloc's decode--BLAS path, results always correct regardless selected backend.

title: Enable or disable the ggml quantized matmul backend — rllm_use_ggml
code: rllm_use_ggml(enable = TRUE)  rllm_backend_enabled()

title: Enable or disable the ggml quantized matmul backend — rllm_use_ggml
text: enable TRUE (default) select "ggml" backend; FALSE restore Rfmalloc's default BLAS path.

title: Enable or disable the ggml quantized matmul backend — rllm_use_ggml
text: Invisibly, TRUE ggml backend active afterwards. rllm_backend_enabled() returns TRUE ggml backend active Rfmalloc matmul backend.

title: Enable or disable the ggml quantized matmul backend — rllm_use_ggml
text: Selection Rfmalloc-scoped; base R's %*% unaffected.

title: Enable or disable the ggml quantized matmul backend — rllm_use_ggml
code: rllm_backend_enabled() #> [1] TRUE rllm_use_ggml(FALSE)   # fall back to Rfmalloc's BLAS decode path rllm_use_ggml(TRUE)    # re-enable ggml quantized products

title: Rllm 0.1.0 (unreleased)
text: Named sibling package explicitly Remotes, dependency installers replace stale monorepo dependencies instead mistaking shared repository commit already installed package. Attaching Rllm quiet. Backend registration remains automatic selected state available rllm_backend_enabled(). Added complete Qwen3.5 execution real Ternary Bonsai 27B metadata tensor layout. serializable R program distinguishes 48 gated-delta recurrent blocks 16 gated-attention blocks, represents recurrent KV state, binds 851 parameters without C++ model branch. Whole-batch incremental paths match pure-R oracle run CUDA plain fmalloc-backed state; 248,320 token-zero logits real checkpoint match portable upstream llama.cpp build bit--bit. Added official GGML group-64 q2_0 quantization typed-product bridge. codec stays encoded Rfmalloc storage uses GGML’s ternary decoder CPU dot product. Added data-architecture program traced ordinary R modules base pipes. preserves several typed inputs, several named operator results, parameter identities, representation taps structured shared loops. rllm_execute() runs dataflow carried loops explicit R lowerings; dense oracle pins Tiny Recursive Model recurrence direct iteration. ESM-2 8M probe records six layers real attention-map contact path. Evo 2 7B probe records 32 HCS, HCM, HCL attention blocks distinct state geometries. checkpoint importers numerical lowerings remain explicit work. GGUF metadata normalized semantic plans llama, LFM2MoE EmbeddingGemma weight borrowed. model-neutral lowerer covers causal, bidirectional symmetric-window attention, normal NEOX RoPE, post-branch RMS normalization, SwiGLU, GEGLU, short convolution, sparse routed experts, mean pooling dense embedding projections. Added rllm_embed() pinned EmbeddingGemma pure-R f32 oracle real 300M Q8_0 model compared upstream GGML execution. Added CUDA transformer path Rggml’s official backend. model-owned context uploads codec-native weights reuses ; mutable inputs, cache state logits use backend-neutral transfer API. RTX 5050 suite pins whole-batch incremental logits, plain fmalloc caches, CPU/CUDA cache handoff. 12-token prompt plus 128 greedy tokens measured median 40.2 tok/s CPU 69.7 tok/s CUDA upload. persistent GGML scheduler graph-capture experiment measured 63.7 62.6 tok/s removed rather retained unused machinery. rllm_gguf_model() now borrows every weight directly read-GGUF mapping. longer copies two-dimensional weights Rfmalloc file dequantizes repacks one-dimensional norms. model retains mapping, GGML receives original encoded pointers. rllm_generate() gained sampling: temperature (0 = greedy, default, existing behaviour unchanged), top_k, top_p (nucleus), seed reproducibility. Greedy stays deterministic; sampled decoding reproducible fixed seed. sampler pure logic logits vector (test_sampling.R). Added incremental decoding KV cache bytes-boundary generation API. rllm_kv_cache() allocates per-layer f32 cache slabs (plain R memory, fmalloc-backed given runtime - cache disk citizen; quantized cache codec can later replace slabs without touching graph). rllm_forward(cache =) appends new keys/values attends everything cached (llama.cpp’s classic layout: K flat, V transposed), advancing n_past reference. rllm_generate() prefills cache decodes greedily one token per step; SmolLM2-135M current CPU path measures 40.2 tok/s 128-token controlled run. correctness invariant - incremental logits equal whole-batch logits every position - pinned synthetic model plain fmalloc cache backings (test_kv_cache.R). model /O boundary bytes, text: rllm_encode()/ rllm_decode() convert raw bytes token ids using GGUF’s byte-level BPE metadata (tokenizer.ggml.tokens/merges, GPT-2 byte alphabet) - external tokenizer; rawToChar() caller’s interpretation. Encoding applies merge ranks without GPT-2’s regex pre-tokenizer, splits may occasionally differ llama.cpp’s canonical ones always decoding back bytes. Real-model record: “capital France Paris. capital Germany ” continues ” Berlin. capital Italy Rome. capital Spain Madrid.” Validated real model (SmolLM2-135M Q4_K_M, 30 layers, GQA 9:3, 272 tensors q4_k/q5_0/q6_k/q8_0/f32 mix): model’s decoded weights, GGML graph matches pure-R reference forward 1e-06 relative (f32-twin roundtrip written Rgguf’s writer), native quantized path agrees argmax; ~0.19 relative logit deviation Q8_K-activation/quantized-weight arithmetic compounded 30 layers, graph error. opt-smoke test (RLLM_TEST_GGUF=<path>, test_real_model.R) exercises loader graph real files without affecting CI/CRAN. q5_0, q5_1, q3_k, q5_k remain accepted quantization typed-GEMM bridge. Rfmalloc codec registration now lives every GGUF storage codec Rgguf uses Rggml’s reference decoder. Added llama-architecture forward pass: rllm_gguf_model() loads GGUF model’s hyperparameters exposes weights native q4_k/f32/… encoding, rllm_forward() assembles GGML compute graph (RMSNorm, RoPE, causal self-attention grouped-query support, SwiGLU) Rggml’s graph-op C-callables weights zero-copy, computing logits every position token batch GGML CPU backend. Quantized weights contracted SIMD-dispatched quantized kernels without ever decoded double. Verified pure-R reference implementation arithmetic synthetic GGUF model written test time - logits agree float accumulation error (< 1e-4 relative) multi-head grouped-query configurations, plus causality probes (test_llama_forward.R). Initial release. Rllm composition layer Rfmalloc ecosystem, wiring together Rfmalloc (file-backed storage), Rgguf (GGUF weights fmalloc tensors) Rggml (vendored GGML compute runtime-SIMD-dispatched quantized kernels). Registers Rggml Rfmalloc codec-aware (“typed”) matrix-multiply backend named "ggml", selected load (toggle rllm_use_ggml()). active, product dense %*% quantized_tensor (tensor fmalloc_tensor GGUF quantized codec: q4_0, q4_1, q8_0, q2_k, q4_k, q6_k) computed natively quantized space: Rfmalloc hands raw compressed payload Rllm, points GGML tensor zero-copy contracts weight row GGML’s SIMD-dispatched vec_dot, quantizing dense operand fly - decode double. Orientations codecs ggml path serve (tensor left, non-quantized codecs) declined fall back Rfmalloc’s decode--BLAS path, results always correct regardless selected backend. rllm_quantize_tensor() encodes dense matrix GGUF quantized block format stores payload Rfmalloc-backed storage, returning fmalloc_tensor - write-side counterpart Rgguf’s gguf_tensor(..., = "native").
